title: Mark agent harnessing as experimental and add Effect-vs-NestJS rationale
trigger: Request to clarify AI-driven workflow positioning and framework rationale
finding: README/docs now label Agent Harnessing as experimental and add a dedicated architecture rationale for choosing Effect over NestJS
followUp: Validate terminology in future onboarding feedback and refine decision doc as architecture evolves

title: Migrate workflow memory to structured store
trigger: Memory bloat risk identified in workflow discussion
finding: Moved from single markdown log to JSONL events plus index/summaries
followUp: Use add-entry script for all future workflow notes

title: Boundary and layer review passed for web-only lint-order patch
trigger: workflow-memory strict coverage reported missing Architecture + ADR Guard for current month
finding: Diff scope is limited to apps/web presenter imports and workflow docs; no package boundary, authz placement, or Effect layer construction changes.
followUp: No ADR required for this cycle.

title: Clarified Effect error assertion guidance
trigger: Uncertainty about toBeInstanceOf vs _tag assertions for Effect tagged errors
finding: Docs now state _tag/field assertions are primary, with toBeInstanceOf optional secondary coverage
followUp: Optionally add a lint/test helper to standardize Effect Exit error assertions

title: Repository guidance drift is reducing agentic reliability
trigger: Requested review of repository/data-access docs for monolithic risk
finding: Repository docs conflict on not-found semantics and Layer construction, while large repo files (300-468 LOC) increase context load.
followUp: Define repo decomposition standard (directory-per-repo with read/write modules), align docs on 404/403 and Layer.succeed vs Layer.effect, add invariant/lint checks for docs-backed rules.

title: Add explicit frontend-design skill without duplicating external prompts
trigger: Request for explicit frontend-design skill while avoiding skill overload
finding: Created a repo-native frontend-design skill that composes existing docs and tanstack-vite guardrails instead of importing external skill verbatim
followUp: Use the skill on next design-heavy apps/web change and refine if repeated review feedback persists

title: Add low-noise React monolith guardrail lint thresholds
trigger: User requested lint rules to prevent god React components without creating a warning flood
finding: Added shared React ESLint guardrails for component size/complexity (function lines, complexity, nesting, JSX depth) with calibrated thresholds that introduce no new current violations.
followUp: Tighten thresholds incrementally after existing large components are refactored, then consider promoting severity to stricter levels.

title: Add single LLM smoke test with full success/error output
trigger: User requested a minimal one-call LLM chat check
finding: Added a dedicated live smoke test that performs one LLM chat-style prompt and prints full success/error output for fast verification.
followUp: Use pnpm test:live:llm:smoke for quick credentials/connectivity checks before running the full live LLM suite.

title: Added URL source option to podcast document selection flows
trigger: User reported missing URL upload option in podcast document select flow
finding: Implemented URL-based document creation in both setup step document picker and workbench add-document dialog, with auto-select/add behavior and cache invalidation consistent with existing upload flow.
followUp: Consider extracting a shared URL uploader component to avoid duplicate form logic across podcast setup and dialog flows.

title: Auto-generate podcasts from deep research documents
trigger: User requested a research checkbox that creates a podcast with default settings when research completes
finding: Added autoGeneratePodcast through document research creation/config persistence and process-research completion flow; when enabled it creates a default 5-minute conversation podcast (Aoede + Charon, no custom instructions) and starts generation without failing research completion if podcast start fails.
followUp: Add a router/integration test that verifies from-research API accepts and persists autoGeneratePodcast in stored researchConfig for end-to-end confidence.

title: Auto-start persona/research chat with bounded follow-ups
trigger: Users reported endless follow-ups and manual final-click friction in persona/deep-research chats
finding: Added bounded follow-up behavior with assistant control tags and auto-triggered create/start flows, plus synthesis fallback for long conversations to improve structured object reliability.
followUp: Add e2e coverage for auto-trigger chat flows once stable fixtures exist for chat streaming.

title: Effect.gen throw guardrail via ESLint
trigger: Question about Effect-idiomatic error signaling and throw usage
finding: Added custom ESLint rule to disallow throw statements directly inside Effect.gen generators; documented fail/die guidance
followUp: Consider extending lint coverage to discourage class-based error assertions as primary checks in Effect tests

title: Enforced tagged-error assertions in Effect use-case tests
trigger: Need to keep Effect error assertions idiomatic and avoid instanceof drift
finding: Migrated use-case/worker tests from toBeInstanceOf to _tag checks and added ESLint rule no-instanceof-in-effect-tests scoped to use-case and worker tests
followUp: Consider expanding the rule to other Effect-heavy test suites once remaining non-use-case tests are migrated

title: Expand CLI LLM command into Gemini playground modes
trigger: Issue #23 requested a terminal tool to test and experiment with Gemini API
finding: Enhanced pnpm cli test llm with smoke test, custom single-prompt mode, and interactive chat REPL while preserving existing command path.
followUp: Add optional non-interactive flags for CI-friendly scripted Gemini smoke checks.

title: Extended tagged-error assertion migration beyond use-cases
trigger: Continue hardening Effect-idiomatic error assertions in backend tests
finding: Migrated remaining backend error toBeInstanceOf assertions to _tag checks (media services, auth, ai map-error, storage in-memory) and added lint rule allowing only built-in instanceof checks (URL/Buffer/AbortSignal)
followUp: Evaluate replacing repeated runPromiseExit+cause extraction with a shared assertion helper to reduce boilerplate

title: Made error-assertion guardrails package-agnostic
trigger: Concern that hardcoded backend package paths would miss future packages
finding: Refactored ESLint rules to generic src/** test globs, added frontend/test-utils opt-outs where intentional, and added invariant test that scans backend tests for forbidden error toBeInstanceOf usage
evidence: tools/eslint/base.js; tools/eslint/custom-rules.js; packages/api/src/server/__tests__/error-assertions.invariants.test.ts; package.json; apps/web/eslint.config.js; packages/ui/eslint.config.js; packages/testing/eslint.config.js
followUp: If new frontend workspaces are added, explicitly opt out of backend-only test rules in workspace eslint config

title: Migrated Vitest runner from workspace file to projects config
trigger: Periodic Scans finding in active quality-closure-loop cycle
finding: Created a dedicated vitest base config, switched root orchestration to test.projects, updated all app/package vitest imports, removed vitest.workspace.ts, and aligned AGENTS/CLAUDE wording.
evidence: vitest.base.config.ts; vitest.config.ts; vitest.workspace.ts; apps/server/vitest.config.ts; apps/web/vitest.config.ts; apps/worker/vitest.config.ts; packages/ai/vitest.config.ts; packages/api/vitest.config.ts; packages/auth/vitest.config.ts; packages/db/vitest.config.ts; packages/media/vitest.config.ts; packages/queue/vitest.config.ts; packages/storage/vitest.config.ts; packages/testing/vitest.config.ts; AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; pnpm typecheck; pnpm test; pnpm test:invariants
followUp: No immediate follow-up; keep root vitest orchestration on test.projects for future upgrades.

title: Resolved web import-order lint regressions in chat and document presenters
trigger: Quality closure lint gate failed with max-warnings=0 due to import/order warnings
finding: Reordered imports in six apps/web components to satisfy import/order without changing behavior.
followUp: Keep import-order cleanup in maintenance passes when refactoring shared chat/document components.

title: Simplified chat, document detail, worker, and effect handler flows with behavior parity
trigger: User requested a point-by-point code-simplifier pass across identified hotspots
finding: Extracted shared chat composer/thread utilities, split oversized document detail UI into focused modules, reduced orchestration complexity in worker/effect-handler paths, and tightened duplicate-heavy tests while preserving behavior via baseline-before/after validation.
followUp: Add a small invariants check that flags new chat surfaces not using shared composer/thread helpers to prevent duplication drift.

title: Smart media export filenames for podcast, voiceover, and infographic downloads
trigger: User request to improve exported file naming across media workflows
finding: Introduced a shared filename builder and wired podcast audio/script, voiceover audio/script, and infographic PNG exports to include normalized title plus variant/date metadata.
followUp: Consider extending the same helper to document exports for full naming consistency across all download surfaces.

title: Split media repos into method modules and standardized Layer.succeed
trigger: Request to improve repository/data access docs and agentic maintainability
finding: Refactored all media repos into smaller method modules, standardized repo layers to Layer.succeed, and aligned style preset not-found with protocol errors.
followUp: Add invariant or lint checks for repo split thresholds and ban inline domain errors in repo files.

title: Tighten chat follow-up cap from 3 to 2
trigger: User requested strict limit of two clarifying follow-ups before auto-start
finding: Reduced shared follow-up cap constant to 2 and aligned persona/research system prompts to enforce max two questions before readiness token.
followUp: Monitor readiness quality after cap reduction and adjust prompt wording if completion quality drops.

title: Wrapped multiline input across all AI chat surfaces
trigger: User reported horizontal scrolling in deep research/persona AI chats
finding: Replaced single-line chat composers with multiline textareas, Enter-to-send with Shift+Enter newline, high maxLength, and break-word rendering so long prompts/messages wrap without horizontal scrolling.
followUp: Consider extracting a shared chat composer component so future AI chat surfaces inherit wrapping and key handling by default.

title: Scope memory coverage hardening and remove react-doctor
trigger: User requested fixing sparse workflow-memory coverage and removing react-doctor
finding: Scoped work to memory tooling/docs/skills only and explicitly excluded unrelated concurrent repo-refactor changes.
followUp: When a workflow skill is invoked, include memory event id in output contract and append event before completion.

title: No blocking perf or cost regressions for web import-order maintenance
trigger: workflow-memory strict coverage reported missing Performance + Cost Guard for current month
finding: Web bundle profile remained stable and there were no backend/worker/AI callsite changes that could affect runtime cost.
evidence: pnpm --filter web build; git diff --name-only (apps/web only)
followUp: Track baseline-browser-mapping package freshness separately from functional regressions.

title: Add monthly workflow-memory coverage baseline scan
trigger: Coverage audit command added to detect sparse workflow memory
finding: Ran coverage scan for 2026-02 and identified missing workflow classes requiring backfill only when those workflows actually ran.
followUp: Run workflow-memory:coverage:strict weekly and convert repeated missing-workflow patterns into guardrails.

title: Frontend and runtime simplification hotspots
trigger: User-requested code simplifier scan
finding: High duplication in chat UI flows and high-complexity orchestration in worker/effect handler paths create maintainability overhead without functional gain.
followUp: Extract shared chat composer primitives and split long orchestration functions into testable helpers while preserving behavior.

title: Quality-closure-loop scan surfaced strict coverage gap and web lint drift
trigger: User requested run quality-closure-loop now
finding: Required gates passed except workflow-memory strict coverage; an additional lint run exposed import-order warnings in six web components.
evidence: pnpm typecheck; pnpm test; pnpm test:invariants; pnpm --filter web build; pnpm workflow-memory:coverage:strict; pnpm lint
followUp: Run missing workflow passes and persist events in the same cycle; keep lint gate in closure checks.

title: Quality loop blocked by missing tooling
trigger: Automation run: quality-closure-loop
finding: Core checks (typecheck, test, lint, invariants, web build) failed to start because node_modules/turbo/vitest were missing and python-dotenv CLI was unavailable.
evidence: pnpm typecheck/test/lint/test:invariants failures; apps/web build error
followUp: Install deps (pnpm install) and python-dotenv[cli] then rerun loop.

title: Quality loop found Vitest workspace-file deprecation warning
trigger: User requested run quality-closure-loop now
finding: All required gates passed, but root vitest runs emitted a deprecation warning for vitest.workspace.ts usage.
evidence: pnpm typecheck; pnpm test; pnpm test:invariants; pnpm --filter web build; pnpm workflow-memory:coverage:strict
followUp: Migrate to test.projects and remove workspace file in the same cycle to avoid future Vitest major-version breakage.

title: Risk review found no correctness/auth/contract regressions in import-only changes
trigger: workflow-memory strict coverage reported missing PR Risk Review for current month
finding: No unsafe casts, streaming-status shortcuts, or API contract changes were introduced; patch is limited to import ordering and docs guardrail text.
evidence: rg -n "as never|as unknown as|status !== 'ready'|eventIterator\(type<unknown>\(\)\)" changed files; pnpm lint; pnpm typecheck; pnpm test
followUp: None.

title: Release readiness go for non-functional lint-order patch with simple rollback
trigger: workflow-memory strict coverage reported missing Release + Incident Response for current month
finding: Go decision for this change class: validation gates passed, rollback is straightforward via commit revert, and no DB/server/worker migration risk exists.
evidence: pnpm typecheck; pnpm test; pnpm test:invariants; pnpm --filter web build; pnpm lint; git diff --name-only (no packages/db apps/server apps/worker changes)
followUp: Ship through normal release train; no incident action needed.

title: Resolved ajv ReDoS advisory via override cleanup
trigger: pnpm audit reported GHSA-2g4f-4pwh-qvx6 on eslint>ajv
finding: Removed eslint-specific ajv@6 override pins so global ajv>=8.18 override applies; audit now clean.

title: Add codebase navigation and debug-fix support skills
trigger: Review of skills-analysis.md requested concrete improvements to skill effectiveness
finding: Added two new repo-specific support skills focused on fast code navigation and test-driven failure narrowing without changing workflow taxonomy.

title: Add quality-closure-loop orchestrator skill for scan-to-fix execution
trigger: User requested a reusable mechanism to run periodic scan findings through execution and closure
finding: Added a new orchestrator skill that chains scan, triage, fix execution, recurrence guardrails, and memory evidence; updated workflow and agent instruction docs so agents can be told to run the loop by name.
followUp: Use quality-closure-loop in the next weekly scan and refine stage timing based on cycle duration and closure rate.

title: Add skills quality checker and slim duplicated skill boilerplate

title: Add workflow-memory coverage audits and remove react-doctor skill

title: Added in-loop closure guardrail for missing workflow coverage
trigger: Repeated strict coverage failures for missing monthly workflow classes during quality closure cycles
finding: Added durable docs rules requiring missing workflows reported by coverage:strict to be executed and recorded in the same active loop before closure.

title: Rename skills without content-studio prefix and add code-simplifier workflow
trigger: User requested code-simplifier skill parity and global skill-name prefix removal

followUp: Use code-simplifier in feature/refactor passes and keep running agent-engine/scripts/sync-skills.sh + pnpm skills:check:strict after any skill edits.

title: Strengthen skill quality checks with procedural/path anchor rules

finding: Added automated checks for procedural section headings and minimum concrete repo path anchors; enriched abstract guard skills with explicit paths.

title: Aligned web export flows on shared smart download naming helper
trigger: apps/web export UX update for clearer downloaded filenames
finding: Updated frontend containers/components to use shared file-download naming logic and added focused tests without changing route/query behavior.

title: Covered new podcast URL document paths with focused component tests
trigger: Frontend flow change touched TanStack Query mutations and tabbed form UI

title: Guardrail auto-trigger chat UX for research/persona dialogs

finding: Introduced shared chat automation state with control-token detection and follow-up caps, then wired one-shot auto-start/auto-create effects in document and persona chat containers (plus setup step).
followUp: Monitor false-positive auto-trigger rate and tune max follow-up cap if conversations feel too short.

title: Standardized AI chat composer wrapping and submission behavior
trigger: Frontend chat UX regression where long prompts scrolled horizontally
finding: Aligned research, persona, writing assistant, and setup research chat inputs on multiline wrapped textareas with consistent Enter submit behavior and centralized max-length/style constants.
followUp: Add a small shared chat-composer component to reduce repeated textarea setup across AI surfaces.

title: Tested research auto-podcast payload in manual and auto-start flows
trigger: Frontend state change needed to keep autoGeneratePodcast in both manual Start Research and assistant auto-start paths
finding: Added focused container/dialog tests to verify checkbox toggling behavior and mutation payload propagation includes autoGeneratePodcast for both flow variants.
followUp: Consider an integration test around the actual dialog component and mutation hook wiring to guard against prop interface regressions.

title: Existing test surface sufficient for non-behavioral import-order edits
trigger: workflow-memory strict coverage reported missing Test Surface Steward for current month
finding: Changes are import reorder only, so no new tests are required; full suite and invariants passed to confirm behavior parity.

title: Added automation operating-model README
trigger: User requested a top-level automations README describing guardrail-first setup and controlled external research evolution

title: Aligned catalog policy wording with current repo dependency practice
trigger: User approved final docs-drift fix for catalog policy mismatch
finding: Updated AGENTS/CLAUDE DX guidance from absolute catalog usage to preferred catalog usage for shared dependencies with explicit-version allowance for package-specific deps, matching current manifests.

followUp: Optionally enforce stricter catalog usage later with CI once manifests are fully migrated.

feedback: Add a docs lint rule for absolute claims (for example 'all' or 'always') when repository checks indicate exceptions.

title: Drift scan found structure, policy, and source-reference mismatches
trigger: User requested repo-wide docs drift review
finding: Found high-confidence drift: docs omit apps/worker in multiple structure sections, AGENTS and CLAUDE diverge on guardrails/skill list, and docs reference removed standards-debate paths. Also, the 'all dependencies use catalog' rule no longer matches current package manifests.
evidence: AGENTS.md:24; CLAUDE.md:24; docs/architecture/overview.md:91; apps/worker/src/worker.ts; AGENTS.md:70; CLAUDE.md:63; AGENTS.md:109; CLAUDE.md:98; docs/README.md:5; docs/debate-decisions.md:3; agent-engine/scripts/standards-debate/ (missing); AGENTS.md:124; CLAUDE.md:122; apps/server/package.json:28; apps/web/package.json:40; packages/media/package.json:55; package.json:54
followUp: Patch AGENTS/CLAUDE/docs/architecture/overview to include worker; resolve AGENTS-vs-CLAUDE bullet drift; either migrate all deps to catalog or narrow wording to policy target scope; remove or replace stale standards-debate source links in docs.
reflection: Mechanical checks (rg + targeted manifest scan) were enough to isolate concrete drift quickly with low ambiguity.
feedback: Add a small CI docs-consistency check for AGENTS/CLAUDE parity and broken local path references in docs.

title: Patched high-confidence docs drift for worker structure and AGENTS/CLAUDE parity
trigger: User approved immediate remediation patch for identified drift items
finding: Added apps/worker to shared structure snippets, synced CLAUDE with AGENTS for preferred skills and Effect error-test guardrail, and removed stale references to deleted agent-engine/scripts/standards-debate paths from active docs.
evidence: AGENTS.md; CLAUDE.md; docs/architecture/overview.md; docs/README.md; docs/debate-decisions.md; diff -u AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md

title: Converted workflow decks to one-page scroll format

title: Hardened approval-executor Docker preflight recovery
trigger: Approved architecture issue execution was blocked by docker.sock permission denial during automation preflight
finding: Updated architecture-approval-executor automation to retry Docker connectivity via context and explicit socket fallbacks before fail-fast, and to include socket/context diagnostics in blocker comments

title: Hardened approval-executor npm DNS preflight recovery
trigger: Issue #30 automation run failed on pnpm install with ENOTFOUND registry.npmjs.org

title: Hardened automation playbooks against non-interactive corepack shim resolution
trigger: Quality Sentinel scans were blocked before execution because non-interactive shell resolution used Node 16 with a Homebrew corepack shim expecting URL.canParse
finding: Updated automation contract, lane playbooks, and quality-closure-loop skill to run Node/pnpm commands via interactive login zsh with an explicit Node>=22.10.0 preflight, removing hardcoded Homebrew PATH fallback.
followUp: Sync wrapper TOML mirrors to ~/.codex if needed and rerun the quality-sentinel lane to confirm end-to-end scan completion.
reflection: Encoding shell context as a first-class preflight avoids environment drift between interactive and scheduled automation runs.
feedback: Avoid hardcoded toolchain paths in playbooks; prefer shell-contract checks plus diagnostics to surface runner drift quickly.

title: Implemented coherent Codex automation contracts and runtime guardrails
trigger: Automation audit required concrete updates to enforce branch-from-main, human approval flow, holistic judge behavior, and Docker/Testcontainers-ready code task execution
finding: Rewrote active Codex automation prompts to enforce a consistent contract: coding-pattern research remains advisory, architecture execution requires thumbs-up approval, self-improvement execution uses a holistic judge with autonomous merge, and code-writing lanes require bootstrap+Docker+DB setup+full gates including e2e before merge

title: Installed Codex automations and removed temporary export bundle

title: Moved automation lane logic to repo playbooks

title: OpenAPI compatibility guard for contracts

finding: Added OpenAPI compatibility test to scan contract output/error schemas for OpenAPI-incompatible types, with documented exceptions for free-form metadata fields.

title: Removed local filesystem paths from automation playbooks
trigger: User required playbook automation instructions to avoid machine-specific local paths and keep host-specific context in wrappers.

followUp: Keep absolute filesystem paths only in automations/codex-app/*/automation.toml wrappers and never in playbook instruction bodies.
reflection: Separating machine-local wrapper context from repo playbook logic improves portability across runners.
feedback: When adding shell examples in playbooks, prefer cwd-relative forms so they remain host-agnostic.

title: Renamed Codex automation lanes for clearer intent
trigger: User approved renaming automation names/ids to improve coherence
finding: Renamed active automation directories and IDs to intention-revealing names: architecture approval executor, harness research radar, self-improvement judge executor, and quality sentinel; updated lane documentation and legacy pointer

title: Simplified quality-sentinel to delegate directly to quality-closure-loop
trigger: User requested rewriting quality-sentinel automation to simply run the quality assurance loop skill
finding: Replaced bespoke sentinel preflight/check/handoff prompt contract with direct delegation to the quality-closure-loop skill and aligned lane docs/runtime mirror wording
followUp: Verify next scheduled quality-sentinel run executes the quality-closure-loop stages and reports workflow-memory event IDs
reflection: Direct skill delegation reduces prompt drift and keeps quality behavior centralized in one maintained skill
feedback: If lane responsibilities shift again, update both repo mirror and ~/.codex runtime docs in the same change

title: Treat workflow-memory dirty files as non-blocking in code-writing automations
trigger: Automation run halted before branching due to expected uncommitted workflow-memory files
finding: Updated code-writing automation playbooks to continue when only agent-engine/workflow-memory/events/*.jsonl and agent-engine/workflow-memory/index.json are dirty, carrying those changes into the run branch instead of blocking for operator choice.
followUp: Monitor the next architecture-approval-executor run to confirm issue-selection execution proceeds without manual dirty-worktree intervention.
reflection: Explicit allowlist-based dirtiness handling removes avoidable human stops while preserving protection for truly unexpected files.
feedback: If more expected generated files appear, extend the allowlist intentionally rather than relaxing all dirty-worktree checks.

title: Codex App automation lanes normalized for coherent orchestration
trigger: User requested an audit and coherent design across Codex App automations with explicit human-gated coding and autonomous judged self-improvement
finding: Identified overlapping automation responsibilities, inconsistent runtime prerequisites, direct-to-main mutation behavior, and missing branch-from-main guarantees; defined a lane model to separate research, human-gated coding-pattern implementation, autonomous judged self-improvement, and QA issue feeding
followUp: Monitor first execution cycle to confirm issue flow: research -> approval/judge -> implementation -> merge

title: Fix lint + CLI typecheck
trigger: QA loop lint/typecheck failures
finding: ESLint v9 config loading broke due to ajv 8.18 incompat; CLI TTS command lacked DOM lib for fetch and wrote Buffer types that failed under strict ArrayBuffer typing.
followUp: Re-evaluate ajv override when eslint/eslintrc updates; ensure node fetch types for CLI remain intentional.

title: QA loop baseline gates clean on 2026-02-21
trigger: User request: run quality assurance loop
finding: Baseline QA gates all passed; no critical/high/medium findings detected in this cycle.
evidence: Commands: pnpm typecheck; pnpm test; pnpm test:invariants; pnpm --filter web build; pnpm workflow-memory:coverage:strict
followUp: Continue routine periodic scans and escalate immediately if any gate regresses.
reflection: Running the full gate set before triage gave a fast, high-confidence health snapshot.
feedback: Keep preserving this command order so failures are localized quickly.

title: QA loop: integration tests need local DB
trigger: Scheduled quality-assurance loop
finding: pnpm test failed with 189 failures because Postgres on :5433 was unavailable, causing createTestContext to throw and ctx.rollback to be undefined.
evidence: pnpm test output shows ECONNREFUSED ::1:5433/127.0.0.1:5433 at packages/testing/src/setup/database.ts:71
followUp: Run pnpm test:db:setup before pnpm test (or document this requirement).

title: Quality sentinel scan clean
trigger: Scheduled quality-sentinel run
finding: All required scan commands passed; only warnings observed.
evidence: pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm test:invariants, pnpm --filter web build, pnpm workflow-memory:coverage:strict
followUp: Monitor recurring warning patterns; consider addressing if they persist.
reflection: Full scan completed without failures.
feedback: None

title: Resolved Node and AJV config blockers
trigger: Quality assurance loop run with required pnpm install + lint gates
finding: Configured repo Node version files and narrowed pnpm AJV override so ESLint lint runs succeed under Node 24.
followUp: Keep AJV security override scoped to v7+ ranges to avoid breaking ESLint v9 eslintrc loader.

title: Add reflection/feedback fields to workflow memory

title: Workflow memory scoring + retrieval helper

trigger: Issue #44 ready-for-dev

title: Required clickable external references in architecture-radar issue policy

title: Tuned architecture-radar references policy to preserve model discretion

finding: Updated issue policy so References are preferred when materially useful, with mandatory real URLs and relevance notes only when external sources are actually cited
followUp: Monitor upcoming architecture-radar issues for balanced use of code examples plus source-backed rationale when needed

title: Adjusted architecture-radar references rule for flexible source usage
trigger: User clarified external research should inform decisions without rigid requirements
finding: Initial strict requirement for References in every issue was too prescriptive; playbook should preserve model discretion while enforcing quality when external sources are cited
followUp: Keep references as context-dependent guidance and require real links only when cited

title: Scoped architecture-radar external reference-link improvement
trigger: User feedback on issue #82 requested architecture-radar issues include concrete external doc links when citing guidance
finding: Current architecture-radar issue contract required core sections but did not explicitly require clickable external URLs, allowing placeholder References text without links
followUp: Update architecture-radar playbook issue policy with a preferred References section and required official/primary URL when external guidance is cited

title: Enforce workflow-memory taxonomy tags for memory and agent diagnostics
trigger: Holistic review of open self-improvement issues showed inconsistent tag semantics for memory and failure analysis

title: Moved automation playbooks and wrapper mirrors under agent-engine

title: Moved repository scripts under agent-engine
trigger: User requested relocating root scripts folder under agent-engine and updating references

title: Consolidate root README into product-first entrypoint
trigger: Request to remove old README companion docs and emphasize product positioning
finding: Replaced engineering-first root README with product-focused messaging and removed redundant README.dev.md/README.product.md
followUp: If onboarding docs still need a dedicated developer guide, reintroduce under docs/ instead of root README variants.
reflection: Kept the documentation change focused and removed dead references in the same pass.

title: Convert README file references to deep links
trigger: Request that README links be deep links instead of plain path text
finding: Updated README surfaces to use repo-relative deep links and removed stale Jenkinsfile references that pointed to missing files
evidence: README.md, docs/README.md, agent-engine/README.md, agent-engine/automations/README.md, apps/web/README.md, docs/spec/generated/README.md, agent-engine/workflow-memory/README.md, jenkins/README.md, research/README.md, tools/cli/README.md

reflection: Applying links repo-wide in one pass kept style and behavior consistent.
feedback: Avoid linking to non-repo runtime files like .env unless they are checked into source.

title: Remove temporary history sections from docs index

finding: Deleted Debate Outcomes Applied and Migration Notes sections from docs/README.md to keep it focused on durable navigation

feedback: Keep docs index pages free of time-bound transition notes.

title: Refocus workflow-memory README on ongoing operations
trigger: User requested removing one-time migration guidance and adding broader repository usage context
finding: Removed migration section from agent-engine/workflow-memory/README.md and added explicit write/read/coverage/compounding integration guidance tied to repo workflows and quality loops.
followUp: Keep one-time migration steps out of operational READMEs and only re-run migration helper manually if manual backfill is needed.
reflection: Placing repo-context guidance near the top makes the purpose of workflow memory clear before schema details.
feedback: When docs mention one-time setup/migration, prefer transient runbooks over permanent README sections.

title: Move workflow-memory store under agent-engine
trigger: Request to relocate workflow-memory and remove legacy-memory references
finding: Moved workflow-memory data from docs/ to agent-engine/, updated scripts and docs/skill/automation references, and removed legacy migration references

title: Add per-workflow catalog with skill and automation mappings

finding: Created agent-engine/workflows with index and one README per workflow including trigger skills, automation entry points, behavior, and execution flow
followUp: Keep workflow pages synchronized when skills, memory keys, or automation lane contracts change
reflection: Single-source workflow pages reduce navigation drift across framework docs

title: Consolidate workflow model around core workflows and generated catalog
trigger: Request to define workflow/skill/automation distinctions and simplify workflow docs
finding: Demoted support workflows to utility skills, added workflows registry + README generator, clarified terminology across framework docs, and converted docs/workflow.md to compatibility entrypoint
followUp: Keep registry as source of truth; run pnpm workflows:generate whenever workflow mappings change

title: Remove docs workflow file and confine process docs to framework

followUp: Keep docs/ limited to engineering standards; keep workflow and memory process docs under agent-engine/

title: Centralized setup docs in root README

title: Replaced architecture-radar with best-practice-researcher random-walk lane
trigger: User requested a rename and behavioral rewrite of architecture-radar
finding: Renamed the lane to best-practice-researcher, rewrote the playbook for stack-wide random-walk best-practice research, enforced memory-driven path diversity between runs, and switched issue labeling to best-practice-researcher.
followUp: Sync the new wrapper to ~/.codex/automations/best-practice-researcher/automation.toml before scheduled runs.
reflection: Treating workflow registry as source of truth and regenerating workflow docs avoided drift.
feedback: Keep compatibility exclusions for legacy labels while old issues remain open.

title: Hardened best-practice-researcher TOML execution prompt
trigger: User requested a stronger wrapper prompt to ensure best-practice-researcher executes correctly
finding: Updated the best-practice-researcher TOML wrapper prompt to require a full run, enforce playbook policy adherence, and require explicit run outputs for path selection, recommendation evidence, dedupe actions, and memory summary; synced runtime wrapper.
followUp: Observe the next run output to ensure the new output contract is consistently followed.
reflection: Keeping execution semantics in the wrapper while deferring policy details to the playbook preserves maintainability.
feedback: If run outputs still drift, move to a stricter checklist template in the playbook.

title: Replaced architecture-approval-executor with ready-for-dev-executor
trigger: User requested replacing architecture-approval-executor with a ready-for-dev executor that can implement 1..N issues.
finding: Renamed the automation lane to ready-for-dev-executor, rewrote its playbook to triage all ready-for-dev issues, select bounded 1..N issue bundles with conservative aggregation, and require explicit workflow routing per selected issue.
followUp: Observe first ready-for-dev-executor run and tune N bundling threshold if scope creeps beyond one coherent PR.
reflection: Using workflow routing as an explicit contract keeps multi-issue aggregation coherent.
feedback: If workflow routing is frequently ambiguous, add issue template fields for intended workflow class.

title: Replaced harness-research-radar with agent-engine-researcher
trigger: User requested renaming harness radar and refocusing it on agent-engine improvements.
finding: Renamed harness-research-radar to agent-engine-researcher, rewrote the playbook to run memory-driven random-walk analysis across workflows/automations/skills/workflow-memory/scripts with optional external harness research, and constrained issue creation to high-confidence improvement findings.
followUp: Observe first run outputs for issue quality and tune domain weights or confidence threshold if noise appears.
reflection: Mirroring best-practice-researcher structure keeps automation lanes consistent while allowing domain-specific focus.
feedback: If candidate issues are too broad, tighten acceptance-criteria template in the playbook.

title: Refined agent-engine-researcher for registry optimization and confirmed 0.8 gate
trigger: User emphasized workflow registry optimization and requested confidence gate set to 0.8.
finding: Updated agent-engine-researcher playbook to explicitly optimize workflow registry mappings and include a Workflow Registry Impact issue section; verified issue creation confidence threshold remains >= 0.8.
followUp: Observe next agent-engine-researcher issues for concrete registry.json optimization proposals.
reflection: Explicit registry impact guidance should improve recommendation precision and reduce vague workflow suggestions.
feedback: If proposals remain broad, require candidate mapping diffs in issue templates.

title: Tightened agent-engine-researcher TOML wrapper prompt
trigger: User asked whether TOML prompt was optimized.
finding: Updated wrapper prompt to explicitly prioritize workflow registry optimization and enforce high-confidence issue actions (>= 0.8), while keeping playbook-first execution contract.
followUp: Monitor next researcher run to confirm improved issue precision and explicit registry-oriented output.
reflection: Making key constraints explicit in wrapper improves runtime consistency without duplicating full playbook.
feedback: If runs still drift, tighten output checklist with required headings.

title: Consolidated execution lanes to ready-for-dev-executor only
trigger: User requested removing self-improvement executor and using one ready-for-dev executor for both researcher streams.
finding: Removed self-improvement-judge-executor lane and rewired workflow/automation mappings so ready-for-dev-executor is the single implementation lane for approved issues from best-practice-researcher and agent-engine-researcher.
followUp: Monitor first consolidated run for issue bundle sizing and workflow-routing quality across mixed researcher issue sets.
reflection: Single executor reduces lane overlap and keeps approval flow consistent.
feedback: If queue depth grows, tune ready-for-dev-executor bundle cap or cadence instead of adding a second executor lane.

title: Added hourly sanity-check periodic-scan automation with direct fix path
trigger: User requested a periodic-scan automation that uses memory-driven focus selection and can directly fix findings with PR + merge.
finding: Created new sanity-check lane with hourly cadence, memory-driven macro-to-micro scan routing, direct fix eligibility gate (confidence >= 0.8), workflow-based execution routing, and auto-merge delivery; wired into workflow registry/docs and runtime sync commands.
followUp: Observe first week of hourly runs and tune scan domain weights or direct-fix eligibility thresholds if signal/noise drifts.
reflection: Combining scan-selection memory with bounded direct-fix criteria keeps autonomy high while controlling scope.
feedback: If PR volume is too high, add a per-day merge cap in sanity-check playbook.

title: Enforced recurrence-prevention gate in sanity-check automation
trigger: User asked to include quality-closure-loop recurrence behavior directly in sanity-check.
finding: Updated sanity-check playbook and wrapper to require recurrence classification, route recurring classes through Self-Improvement, and block auto-merge unless a concrete prevention artifact ships for recurring findings.
followUp: Observe upcoming sanity-check runs to ensure recurring findings consistently include prevention artifacts and recurrence evidence in PR output.
reflection: Embedding recurrence policy directly in the autonomous scan lane reduces dependence on manual orchestration.
feedback: If recurrence matching is noisy, add stricter pattern-matching guidance and taxonomy tags in playbook.
